titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Eugene Victor Rostow papers
titlestmt.author: compiled by staff of Manuscripts and Archives; revised June 2022 by Michelle Peralta

controlnote.p: This encoded finding aid is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0.
This collection contains 4 published digital objects.

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Friday November 29, 2024 at 04:23

did.abstract: The papers consist of correspondence, subject files, writings, printed material, newspaper clippings, photographs, and memorabilia documenting the professional and personal life of Eugene V. Rostow, Yale Law School professor and dean.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

Original audiovisual materials, as well as preservation and duplicating masters, may not be played. Researchers must consult use copies, or if none exist must pay for a use copy, which is retained by the repository. Researchers wishing to obtain an additional copy of non-commercially produced items for their personal use should consult Copying Services information on the Manuscripts and Archives web site.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Gift of Eugene V. Rostow, 1979, 1981, 1984, 1990, and 2000. Gift of the Estate of Eugene V. Rostow, 2003 and 2023. Transferred from the Yale Law School, 1982, and 2021.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Copyright status of collection materials is unknown. Transmission or reproduction of materials protected by U.S. Copyright Law (Title 17, U.S.C.) beyond that allowed by fair use requires the written permission of the copyright owners. Works not in the public domain cannot be commercially exploited without permission of the copyright owners. Responsibility for any use rests exclusively with the user.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Eugene Victor Rostow Papers (MS 1024). Manuscripts and Archives, Yale University Library.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Eugene V. Rostow (1913-2002) graduated from Yale College in 1933 and Yale Law School in 1937. During his career, he served on the law school faculty and as dean. Rostow also held various governmental positions including that of undersecretary for political affairs during the Lyndon Baines Johnson administration and director of the Arms Control and Disarmament Agency under President Reagan. He was a leader of the Coalition for a Democratic Majority and was a founder of the Committee on the Present Danger. He published widely in the fields of foreign relations and international security.
Rostow died on November 25, 2002, in Alexandria, Virginia.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The papers consist of correspondence, subject files, writings, printed material, newspaper clippings, photographs, and memorabilia documenting the professional and personal life of Eugene V. Rostow, Yale Law School professor and dean.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The records are arranged by accession.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Copies of commercially produced audiovisual materials contained in this collection cannot be made for researcher use outside of the repository.

did.unittitle: "Oil Book" (A National Policy for the Oil Industry)
"Committee" (Committee on Curriculum and Personnel, Yale Law School)
"The Association of the Bar of the City of New York (Centennial Comm.)"
Centennial Committee (Prospectus - New York bar - Merrel Clark)
Articles and letters to the editor
News clippings about E. V. Rostow
Report on plans for Europe -America Conference, 1973
Steering committee meeting minutes for committee to organize America -Europe Conference, 1973
Correspondence, steering committee for Europe -America Conference, 1973
Personal - Bills, Accts., Letters on insurance
Correspondence, notes for speeches and lectures
UN Conference on Road and Motor Transport

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Material (1938-1959) is primarily correspondence to and from Rostow, from a variety of people and on a range of subjects. There are also small amounts of writings (speeches and reviews) and printed material (clippings). There is also one photograph of Rostow.

custodhist.head: Custodial History
custodhist.p: Received April 23, 1982 as transfer from the Law School.

did.unittitle: Notes, drafts, writings, interviews, speeches, professional correspondence, and printed material
Reviews of The Ideal in Law
Subject files: Writings, National Defense University, Middle East

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Original audiovisual materials, as well as preservation and duplicating masters, may not be played. Researchers must consult use copies, or if none exist must pay for a use copy, which is retained by the repository. Researchers wishing to obtain an additional copy for their personal use should consult Copying Services information on the Manuscripts and Archives web site.

did.unittitle: America and the World: A Conversation with Stanley Hoffmann On American Foreign Policy in a World Transformed , Council on Foreign Relations, Inc.

title.part: On the Line #1-00126: The U.S., Japan and the World

accessrestrict.p: As a preservation measure, original materials, as well as preservation and duplicating masters, may not be used. Digital access copies must be provided for use. Contact Manuscripts and Archives at beinecke.library@yale.edu to request access.

accessrestrict.p: As a preservation measure, original materials, as well as preservation and duplicating masters, may not be used. Digital access copies must be provided for use. Contact Manuscripts and Archives at beinecke.library@yale.edu to request access.

accessrestrict.p: As a preservation measure, original materials, as well as preservation and duplicating masters, may not be used. Digital access copies must be provided for use. Contact Manuscripts and Archives at beinecke.library@yale.edu to request access.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: As a preservation measure, original materials, as well as preservation and duplicating masters, may not be used. Digital access copies must be provided for use. Contact Manuscripts and Archives at beinecke.library@yale.edu to request access.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Gift of Estate of Eugene V. Rostow, 2003.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Materials in the accession primarily document Rostow's professional correspondence, writings, and speeches. While topical files include materials on anti-trust, Détente, the Arms Control and Disarmament Agency (ACDA), and the Committee on the Present Danger, researchers may also find Rostow's correspondence useful in detailing his thoughts and shifting of perspectives on political issues. A small selection of teaching files, personal correspondence, primarily with his brother Walt Whitman Rostow, and photographs have been included.
The majority of original folder titles have been retained.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials have been organized into the following groupings: Correspondence; Writings, reports and speeches; Subject files; and Photographs.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: A photograph of the Yale Institute of International Affairs discussion group with Rostow, circa late 1930s or early 1940s, received in 2022 (as accession 2023-m-0004) was added to Box 22, folder 13.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Materials include letters, postcards, and cables written to and by Eugene V. Rostow. Correspondents may be found across multiple folders.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials are arranged chronologically and alphabetically therein.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This selection of materials contains a gap of folders, approximately letters E-M which were not present in the collection at the time of transfer.

did.unittitle: Buckley, James L. (Undersecretary of State for Security Affairs)
Casey, William J. (Interim Foreign Policy Advisory Board)
The Christian Science Monitor (Joseph C. Harsch)
de Villiers, C. F. (Foreign Affairs Association)
Reagan, Ronald W. - Correspondence related to

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Primarily pertains to the donation of Dr. Sachs papers to Yale University Library.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes materials written in Spanish.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains papers and reports.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Materials include writings, reports, articles, and speech transcripts written or delivered by Eugene V. Rostow.

did.unittitle: "Competing Systems of Corporate Reorganization: Chapters X and XI of the Bankruptcy Act"
Glenn, Garrard, Fraudulent Conveyances and Preferences - Book review
"The Bituminous Coal Act: Bituminous Coal and the Public Interrest; Joinder in Demurrer"
Dorfman, Joseph, The Economic Mind in American Civilization, 1606-1865 - Book review
Lynch, David, The Concentation of Economic Power - Book review
"The Partition of Germany and the Unity of Europe"
"Monopoly Under the Sherman Act" with reply and rejoinder
"The Study of Economics and Education in Law"
"Entry into the Oil Refining Business: Vertical Integration Re-examined"

Law Journal Banquet, Dean Rostow's Remarks
"The Illegal Premise of the Loyalty-Security Programs," draft
"Interplay of Fiscal and Monetary Policy" - Remarks at the Annual Conference of the Iowa Bankers Association
"Negotiating a Berlin Settlement: A 'European' Approach to Germany's Future" and "Will the Liberals Repeat Their Mistakes?
New York State Bar meetings - Remarks
"Seen from the Cam: Reflections of a Wandering Dean"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes report of the Dean of the Yale Law School (1962).

did.unittitle: "Law, City Planning, and Social Action," with Edna G. Rostow
Prospects for the Alliance - Speech at American Society for Friendship with Switzerland
"The Civil Rights and Immigration Legislation of 1964-1965 in Perspective"

President's Task Force on Communications Policy - Final report

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes "The Vocabulary of Foreign Policy" and untitled drafts

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains "Reflections on the Impasse in the INF Talks"; "Walk-Out in Geneva"; "Equality versus 'Equal Security': The Soviet Gambit at Geneva"; "Nuclear Impasse in Geneva"; and "The Soviet Gambit in Geneva."

did.unittitle: "Equality versus 'Equal Security': The Soviet Gambit at Geneva," revised

"Social Policy and Social Unrest in Germany"
World Politics and World Trade - Book reviews

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Materials include Eugene V. Rostow's subject files, teaching files, and biographical materials.

did.unittitle: Chappell, Herbert, "A Review of Professor Eugene Rostow's 'A National Policy for the Oil Industry'"
Graveson, Ronald H., "A Review of 'The Sovereign Perogrative: The Supreme Court and the Quest for Law' by Eugene V. Rostow"
Attorney General's National Committee to Study the Antitrust Laws - Report of the Legal and Economic Concepts Work Group

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains Rostow's statement before the subcommittee on antitrust and monopoly of the committee on the Judiciary, United States Senate, concerning S. 1552.

did.unittitle: Testimony of Dr. William J. Baumol
Hamson, C. J., "The Rule of Law in the West"
Committee on the Present Danger - Radio program transcript and memo
Petro Gulf Baker Ranch/Petro Development Corp. - South Sacatosa Corp. (Peter Behr)
Arms Control (Arms Control and Disarmament Agency)
Remarks by Judge Shirley Hufstedler on the presentation of the Earl Warren medal to Eugene V. Rostow

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Materials contains reading lists and course syllabi for classes Rostow taught as a Yale Law School faculty member. Also included are faculty meeting notes and memoranda.

did.unittitle: Manual for Moot Court, Yale Law School

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Materials document Rostow's personal life and include materials collected and created by Rostow as a student, newspaper clippings and interviews about Rostow, as well as Rostow's curriculum vitae.

did.unittitle: Roscoe Pound (University of Nebraska) lectures - Press releases
University of New Haven, honorary degree citation
North Atlantic Trade Organization (NATO) - Press conference
Interview with Michael Charlton for "Analysis" - Transcript

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Materials include images of professional events, the Yale Law School faculty, and personal photographs.

did.unittitle: Rostow, Eugene V., and President Lyndon B. Johnson
Rostow, Eugene V., and President Ronald Reagan
